title: The Binomial Distribution Process
description: A flowchart illustrating the key components, assumptions, and process of a Binomial experiment, leading to the Binomial probability formula.
elements: A fixed number (n) of independent trials
Each trial has two outcomes: Success (p) or Failure (1-p)
Binomial Probability Formula: P(X=k) = C(n,k) * p^k * (1-p)^(n-k)
Result: Binomial Distribution (plot of P(X=k) vs. k)
relationships: The process begins with defining the experiment's fixed parameters (n, p).
Multiple independent trials are conducted, each with a binary outcome.
The count of successes (k) from these n trials is the random variable.
This count is input into the Binomial formula to calculate its probability.
Repeating this for all possible k (0 to n) generates the full Binomial distribution.
